summary: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) system testing complete. Backend: 16/16 tests passed for granular permissions (read/write/none per module). Frontend: User Management page with permission dropdowns working correctly. Permission enforcement verified - users with sales:read can view but not create sales; users with expenses:none blocked from accessing expenses.

issues: Console shows hydration warnings about <span> in <tbody> - likely from Emergent's visual editor wrapper, doesn't affect functionality

critical_code_review_comments: database.py has_permission() (line 90-114) - Correctly checks admin role first, then normalizes permissions and validates level (read/write)
database.py require_permission() (line 134-151) - Raises HTTPException 403 with clear error message on permission denied
database.py get_branch_filter() (line 117-131) - Returns empty dict for admins, branch_id filter for restricted users
models.py User.permissions (line 23) - Uses Dict[str, str] type with field_validator to normalize old list format to new dict format
UsersPage.jsx (line 16-43) - ALL_MODULES array defines all modules with group categorization for permission UI
UsersPage.jsx (line 55-65) - normalizePermissions() handles backward compatibility with old list format
UsersPage.jsx (line 361-379) - Each permission dropdown shows None/Read/Write options with color-coded badges
DashboardLayout.jsx (line 103-121) - normalizePermissions() and hasPermission() used for navigation filtering
seed_data_creation: Test users created and cleaned up during tests. Limited User exists with 1 write and 1 read permission for permission enforcement testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: RBAC system fully tested. Key test data-testids: users-page-title, add-user-button, user-dialog, user-name-input, user-email-input, user-password-input, user-role-select, user-branch-select, permission-{module} (e.g., permission-sales), submit-user-button, users-table, user-row, edit-user-button, delete-user-button. Permission format is dict-based: {'sales': 'write', 'expenses': 'read', 'customers': 'none'}. Backend uses require_permission() helper for enforcement. Frontend hasPermission() in DashboardLayout filters navigation. Limited User account exists for testing (test.limited@ssc.com / test123).
